sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The SPACE action caused a swap between the left and right panels:
- Before: Left panel had the black "7" shape (id=4) with white dots. Right panel had the dark gray "L" (id=5) and yellow "L" (id=11).
- After: The black "7" shape (now id=5) moved to the LEFT panel (children of id=0). The dark gray "L" (now id=6) and yellow "L" (now id=12) moved to the RIGHT panel (children of id=2).
Wait, let me re-read this more carefully:
- Before: id=0 (left blue bg) had children=[4] (black "7" shape)
- After: id=0 (left blue bg) has children=[5] (black "7" shape)
- Before: id=2 (right blue bg) had children=[5, 11] (dark gray "L" and yellow "L")
- After: id=2 (right blue bg) has children=[6, 12] (dark gray "L" and yellow "L")
So the objects stayed in the same panels but their IDs changed. Let me check if positions actually changed by looking at the boundaries.
Actually wait - the colors also changed!
- Before: id=3 was Y (yellow strip), id=12 was B (black strip)
- After: id=3 is B (black strip), id=4 is Y (yellow strip - but now 63 pixels)
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me look at the actual ASCII to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me compare the ASCII representations to see actual pixel changes
prev_ascii = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

diffs = []
for i in range(min(len(prev_ascii), len(curr_ascii))):
    if prev_ascii[i] != curr_ascii[i]:
        diffs.append(i)
        
print(f"Lines that changed: {diffs[:20]}")
if diffs:
    for line_num in diffs[:10]:
        print(f"Line {line_num}:")
        print(f"  Before: {prev_ascii[line_num]}")
        print(f"  After:  {curr_ascii[line_num]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
